meta.title: Lone Working Risk Assessment — <role>, <site>
meta.subtitle: <solitary activity> · eliminate-the-solitary-exposure-first · check-in + missed-check-in escalation · coverage-checked comms
meta.author: HSE Renewables
meta.classification: Internal — competent-person (lone-working / renewables-field SME) review required
meta.deid_notice: The lone worker is referred to by role label; the check-in / escalation responder by role. Personal contact numbers, the shift pattern and the precise work/home location were pseudonymised before drafting and are held in a separate access-controlled operational record; the re-identification key is held separately and is not part of this document.

sections.text: Scope: the specific solitary activity performed by the named role at the named site. The proposed control is recorded and tested against the eliminate-the-solitary-exposure-first + coverage-checked-comms + check-in/escalation discipline.

metrics.label: Comms coverage at the work location
metrics.caption: no signal treated as a control failure

sections.text: 2. Routing gate (work at height / electrical routed, not assessed solo)

Lone work at height routes to wind-turbine-work-at-height-rescue (REN-01)'s two-person, tested-rescue baseline — a solo climb is not acceptable and 'call 999' is never the rescue plan. Lone electrical work routes to the cross-listed utilities skills (arc-flash-assessment / live-working-risk-assessment). Those pointers are recorded, never rebuilt here.
A lone-worker device supplements — never replaces — the procedure, and 'no mobile signal' is a control failure, not an accepted residual risk.
3. Control order (eliminate the solitary exposure first)

rows.control: Eliminate the solitary exposure — pair up / re-schedule so the worker is no longer alone (LEAD control)
Substitute / engineer — remote monitoring; a coverage-checked communication path at the work location
Administrative — scheduled check-in + a defined missed-check-in escalation path (responder / method / response time)
Device (residual supplement only) — a BS 8484:2022-conformant device + monitored response chain, on top of the procedure, never instead of it

sections.text: No lone-working assessment is defensible where the solitary exposure can be eliminated and is not, or where a device is treated as the control instead of a coverage-checked check-in + escalation procedure.

rows: [GAP] until confirmed; no-signal is a control failure
sections.caption: The communication + check-in + escalation arrangement (INDG73 / BS 8484)
Residual risk scored after the lead controls are applied (5x5)

sections.text: Lone work at height routes to wind-turbine-work-at-height-rescue (REN-01); lone electrical work routes to the cross-listed utilities skills — neither is assessed solo here.

items: HSE INDG73 (Protecting lone workers, rev 4): risk-assessment duty, reliable coverage-checked communication, scheduled check-ins + a missed-check-in escalation procedure, violence + stress/wellbeing
BS 8484:2022 (Provision of lone worker device services): a device supplements, never replaces, the procedure
Work at Height Regulations 2005 reg 4 (lone WAH routes to REN-01's two-person / tested-rescue baseline)
ISO 45001 cl 6.1.2 — hazard ID & risk assessment; hierarchy of controls
